methodsAttempted.query: site:ul.ie/provost/functions-processes/academic-integrity-unit "Academic Misconduct and Gen AI" "University of Limerick"

Direct HTTP checks returned 200 for the verified UL Academic Integrity Unit pages. The UL robots.txt does not disallow these public policy paths.

artifacts.summary: Official University of Limerick sources were discovered with Firecrawl search and fetched with Firecrawl plus direct HTTP checks. Claims are limited to UL Academic Integrity Unit pages that state GenAI assessment, academic-integrity, tool-approval, or data-handling guidance; broader integrity and toolkit pages are recorded as rejected context.

artifacts.claimText: University of Limerick guidance says legitimate AI use in academia is a local decision, with module coordinators, programme leads, or Heads of Department setting limits within module or programme design.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Limerick assessment guidance states that use of AI in an unauthorized manner is a breach of academic integrity, and that use not permitted by the module leader is a breach of academic integrity.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Limerick guidance says GenAI use in assessment beyond the specifications of the module lead or programme lead is considered academic misconduct and related concerns should be managed under the procedures for academic misconduct allegations.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UL GenAI principles state that any tool used within the university must comply with UL data protection, IT security, and acceptable usage policies, and that cloud tools must be approved through the Cloud Governance process.

citation.citationTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence Principles for the University, Staff, Researchers and Students
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UL student guidance says Microsoft Copilot accessed through UL staff or student accounts is currently the preferred GenAI platform from a security and data integrity standpoint when GenAI is used by staff or students.

citation.citationTitle: Tips for Students on the use of Gen AI
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UL guidance states that outputs from GenAI tools relied on for essential information, decision-making, or research must be independently verified.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Given the variety of offerings, disciplines and courses, the question of legitimate use of AI within academia, will inevitably be a local decision. That is, a module coordinator, programme lead or Head of Department, will dictate the limits of legitimate uses of AI within module or programme design, in keeping with the module or programme aims and content.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Is the use of Gen AI within academia legitimate?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The Academic Integrity Policy and the Procedures for Managing Allegations of Academic Misconduct, available on the UL Policy hub, indicate that it is a breach of academic integrity if AI is used in an unauthorised manner. In other words, it is the responsibility of the module leader/equivalent to decide and communicate the level of acceptable use of AI to students i.e. to determine what is authorised/unauthorised use.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: What is the University's position on the use of AI for teaching, learning and assessment activities?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of Gen AI in assessment beyond the specifications of the module lead and/or programme lead is considered to be a form of academic misconduct, as described in the Academic Integrity Policy. Concerns relating to the unauthorised use of GenAI should be managed in accordance with the Procedures for Managing Allegations of Academic Misconduct.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: What is the protocol for managing breaches of the guidance given to students on the use of Gen AI in assessment?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence Principles for the University, Staff, Researchers and Students
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of any tool within the University must be compliant with the UL Data Protection Policy, IT Security Policy, and the Acceptable Usage Policy. All tools being used within the University must go through the appropriate approval processes. Tools with a cloud aspect must be approved through the Cloud Governance process.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Tips for Students on the use of Gen AI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: From a security and data integrity standpoint, if Gen AI is to be used by staff or students, the preferred platform is currently Microsoft Copilot, accessed through UL staff or student accounts. UL maintains a contractual agreement with Microsoft, a distinction not shared with other providers.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: What Gen AI tools can be used by students?

artifacts.title: University of Limerick Generative AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official University of Limerick Academic Integrity Unit pages provide GenAI guidance for assessment, academic misconduct handling, tool approval, data protection, and preferred student/staff platform use. No single central binding AI policy was found in the public crawl; the strongest evidence is a cluster of official guidance pages and references to Academic Integrity Policy procedures.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No central binding public University of Limerick AI policy document was found; claims are scoped to official Academic Integrity Unit guidance pages fetched in this run.
SharePoint, Workvivo, Brightspace, and service-portal linked documents were not accessed where they appeared to be login-protected, template-oriented, or outside the public-source crawl boundary.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
